preamble.title: noctis/cardano-contracts
preamble.description: Noctis Protocol — Cardano L1 smart contracts (Aiken)

anyOf.title: Some
anyOf.description: An optional value.

anyOf.title: None
anyOf.description: Nothing.

anyOf.title: Some
anyOf.description: An optional value.

anyOf.title: None
anyOf.description: Nothing.

anyOf.title: Some
anyOf.description: An optional value.

anyOf.title: None
anyOf.description: Nothing.

bonding_curve/BondingCurveDatum.title: BondingCurveDatum
bonding_curve/BondingCurveDatum.description: The datum stored at the bonding curve UTxO

bonding_curve/BondingCurveRedeemer.title: BondingCurveRedeemer
bonding_curve/BondingCurveRedeemer.description: Redeemer actions — what the contract can be spent with

anyOf.title: ActivateCurve
anyOf.description:
Governor activates the curve (Inactive -> Active). `current_timestamp`
 is stamped into `activated_at`, verified against the tx's actual
 validity range (T29) so ExpireCurve has a trustworthy start point.

anyOf.title: BuyTokens
anyOf.description:
Public buy — no identity proof required, unlike Tier B/C's DarkVeil-
 gated purchases. The caller supplies the expected price and fee split
 (computed off-chain) and the contract verifies both.

anyOf.title: ClaimCreatorFees
anyOf.description:
Creator claims accrued creator fees (Stream A pattern — pre-graduation
 bonding curve fees only; distinct from post-graduation LP trading fees).

 T107 fix (2026-07-19): two changes, found during pre-test security
 review ahead of a live Preprod fee-claim test. (1) Previously had NO
 value-conservation check at all — only the datum's accrued counter was
 verified, not that the curve's real lovelace balance actually
 decreased by `amount` or that `amount` reached the claimer. Since the
 signer must BE the fee recipient, this wasn't a third-party theft
 risk, but it let that signer construct a transaction draining the
 ENTIRE curve balance (buyers' `total_raised` principal included)
 while the datum showed only a token-sized `amount` decrement — fixed
 via the same real-value-movement discipline every other value-moving
 redeemer in this file already uses (`payment_received`,
 `lovelace_paid_from_curve`, `graduation_funds_left_curve`). (2) New
 `platform_claim_fee` — a real, on-chain-enforced flat charge (in
 addition to `amount`, paid INTO the curve, not out of it) covering
 this claim, split 40/60 ops/treasury into the existing
 `ops_fees_accrued`/`treasury_fees_accrued` accumulators rather than a
 new payout address. Approximates CLAUDE.md's existing
 `STAKING_CLAIM_FEE_USD` ($1) precedent but — unlike that fee, which is
 advisory-only in `staking_pool.ak` — this one is a real on-chain
 minimum, not just a client convention. Aiken has no in-circuit oracle
 access (same architectural constraint Compact has), so
 `min_platform_claim_fee_lovelace` below is a deliberately conservative
 SANITY FLOOR, not a precise live $1 peg — the real $1-equivalent is
 computed off-chain via the already-real `usdToMinAdaLovelace()`
 Orcfax oracle (`integration/ada-price-oracle.ts`) and paid with
 margin above the floor in the normal case.

anyOf.title: ClaimTreasuryFees
anyOf.description:
Governor claims accrued treasury fees.

 T107 fix: same missing value-conservation gap as ClaimCreatorFees,
 fixed the same way — no new fee added here (the governor is claiming
 treasury's OWN existing accrual, not paying a new platform charge).

anyOf.title: ClaimOpsFees
anyOf.description: Governor claims accrued ops fees. T107 fix: same as ClaimTreasuryFees.

anyOf.title: SellTokens
anyOf.description:
Public sell — the reverse of BuyTokens (T106, 2026-07-19). Symmetric
 pricing: BuyTokens prices a batch at the PRE-trade `tokens_sold` (the
 low edge of the range being added); SellTokens prices at the
 POST-trade `tokens_sold` (`datum.tokens_sold - token_amount` — the low
 edge of the range being removed, the SAME point). This makes an
 immediate buy-then-sell of the same amount net to zero before fees —
 the 2.0% round-trip fee is the only intentional friction, not a
 pricing-formula asymmetry. Verified by working through the invariant
 `total_raised + creator_fees_accrued + treasury_fees_accrued +
 ops_fees_accrued == curve's real lovelace balance` (true after every
 BuyTokens call) and confirming it still holds after SellTokens:
 `total_raised` decreases by the FULL `gross_proceeds` (not just
 `net_proceeds`), while `fee_total` accrues to the same 3 accumulators
 exactly as a buy's fee does — only `net_proceeds` (gross minus fees)
 actually leaves the curve to pay the seller.

 A seller can only sell up to their own tracked `per_address_purchases`
 total via THIS contract — deliberate scope, not a bug:
 `per_address_purchases` only tracks purchases made through BuyTokens,
 so tokens a wallet acquired via a direct secondary transfer aren't
 tracked here and can't be sold back through this redeemer. This also
 means the creator (permanently blocked from BuyTokens by T32, so
 their tracked purchase total is always 0) can never sell here either
 — no separate creator-block needed, it falls out of this check for
 free. Selling decrements the same tracked total BuyTokens increments,
 freeing wallet_cap headroom for future buys — this contract treats
 the cap as "current net curve position," not "lifetime gross
 purchases."

anyOf.title: CancelCurve
anyOf.description: Cancel the launch (governor only, failure path — voluntary, any time)

anyOf.title: ExpireCurve
anyOf.description:
Permissionless — anyone can force-cancel a curve that's been Active
 for longer than `max_curve_duration` without reaching Graduated
 (T29). No signature required, same trust model as
 cto_governance.ak's `ExpireProposal`: the deadline check itself is
 the authorization.

anyOf.title: ClaimBuyback
anyOf.description:
Pro-rata buyback (T48, 2026-07-10): once Cancelled, a token holder
 returns `token_amount` of the launch token to this contract and
 reclaims their proportional share of the stranded ADA principal
 (`total_raised * token_amount / tokens_sold`, using the curve's own
 real division — Aiken's Int is arbitrary-precision, no ZK-circuit
 division restriction the way Compact has). The buyer must actually
 send the tokens in the same transaction (`tokens_returned_to_curve`)
 and actually receive the computed ADA share
 (`lovelace_paid_from_curve`) — same real-value-movement pattern as
 `payment_received`/`token_delivered`, not a self-reported claim.

anyOf.title: Graduate
anyOf.description:
T49: seed the LP once Graduated. Permissionless — same idiom as
 ExpireCurve, since every effect is verified against real value
 movement, not a signature-backed claim: nobody benefits from calling
 this "incorrectly" because a malformed attempt simply fails the
 invariant checks below. Moves `total_raised` lovelace and
 `lp_reserve_tokens` of the launch token to `lp_escrow_credential`;
 fee accumulators are untouched and remain separately claimable.

anyOf.title: TriggerCTO
anyOf.description:
Trigger CTO — governor only, called after a SilenceLockTrigger
 proposal passes on cto_governance.ak. Redirects future
 ClaimCreatorFees claims to community_pub_key_hash. Same pattern as
 lp_escrow.ak's TriggerCTO (T13).

anyOf.title: DissolveCTO
anyOf.description:
Dissolve CTO — governor only, called after a DissolveCTO proposal
 passes (creator returned). Reverts ClaimCreatorFees back to the
 original creator.

anyOf.title: QueryState
anyOf.description:
Read-only query, no state change — off-chain callers should just read
 the datum directly rather than spend through this redeemer

anyOf.title: ActivateCurve
anyOf.description:
`current_timestamp` stamped into `activated_at`, verified against the
 tx's validity range (T29, same pattern as Tier A).

anyOf.title: ClaimDarkVeilTokens
anyOf.description:
T46/privacy fix (2026-07-11): settles a private DarkVeil purchase for
 real — charges the flat DV price in ADA, delivers the tokens, and
 consumes the buyer's own Merkle leaf without ever revealing anyone
 else's allocation. See file header.

anyOf.title: ClaimCreatorFees
anyOf.description:
T108 fix (2026-07-21): (1) previously only the datum counter was
 checked — no verification that real lovelace ever left the curve's
 UTXO and reached the claimer, an identical gap to Tier A's T107,
 letting the signer drain the ENTIRE curve balance (buyers' principal
 included) while the datum showed only a small decrement. Fixed via
 the same real-value-movement discipline every other value-moving
 redeemer in this file already uses (`payment_received`,
 `buyback_share_paid`, `graduation_funds_left_curve`). (2) New
 `platform_claim_fee` — ported for parity with Tier A's T107 fix
 (confirmed 2026-07-21): a real, on-chain-enforced flat charge (in
 addition to `amount`, paid INTO the curve, not out of it), split
 40/60 ops/treasury into the existing `ops_fees_accrued`/
 `treasury_fees_accrued` accumulators. Same deliberately conservative
 sanity-floor approach as Tier A (no in-circuit oracle access) — see
 `min_platform_claim_fee_lovelace`'s own comment.

anyOf.title: ClaimTreasuryFees
anyOf.description:
T108 fix: same missing value-conservation gap as ClaimCreatorFees,
 fixed the same way — no new fee added here (the governor is claiming
 treasury's OWN existing accrual, not paying a new platform charge).

anyOf.title: ClaimOpsFees
anyOf.description: T108 fix: same as ClaimTreasuryFees.

anyOf.title: ExpireCurve
anyOf.description:
Permissionless force-cancel once Active for longer than
 `max_curve_duration` without reaching Graduated (T29) — same idiom
 as Tier A's ExpireCurve.

anyOf.title: ClaimBuyback
anyOf.description: Pro-rata buyback (T48) — same mechanism as Tier A's ClaimBuyback.

anyOf.title: Graduate
anyOf.description: T49: seed the LP once Graduated — same mechanism as Tier A's Graduate.

anyOf.title: TriggerCTO
anyOf.description:
CTO fee-redirect fix (2026-07-12) — same mechanism as Tier A's
 bonding_curve.ak.

anyOf.title: AnchorDvAllocationRoot
anyOf.description:
T112 fix (2026-07-19, Tier B cross-chain audit): anchors the real
 dv_allocation_root computed off-chain by the governor from
 eligibility_gate.compact's dvTokensPurchased map, once DarkVeil has
 closed on Midnight. Governor-signed — same trust boundary as
 ActivateCurve; no open-relay/censorship-resistance need here, unlike
 cto_governance.ak's AnchorVoteResult (T111), since there's no
 community vote this value could be used to suppress. Gated on
 curve_state == Inactive, so it can only run before public trading
 opens and can be safely re-called (overwriting a mistaken root) any
 number of times while still Inactive — ClaimDarkVeilTokens itself
 requires curve_state == Active, so no claim could ever have processed
 against a not-yet-final root. See file header and
 internal tracking.

bonding_curve_tier_b/MerkleProofStep.title: MerkleProofStep
bonding_curve_tier_b/MerkleProofStep.description:
One step of a Merkle inclusion proof — same `(sibling, direction)` shape
 already used by the Compact contracts' allowlist/balance-snapshot
 proofs, just typed for Aiken instead.

cardano/address/Credential.title: Credential
cardano/address/Credential.description:
A general structure for representing an on-chain `Credential`.

 Credentials are always one of two kinds: a direct public/private key
 pair, or a script (native or Plutus).

cto_governance/CtoAnchorState.title: CtoAnchorState
cto_governance/CtoAnchorState.description: CTO lifecycle states on Cardano L1

anyOf.title: PreCTO
anyOf.description: No CTO activity yet — creator is active

anyOf.title: CTOTriggered
anyOf.description: A CTO vote has been anchored — the community voted to trigger CTO

anyOf.title: CTODissolved
anyOf.description: CTO was dissolved — creator returned and community voted to dissolve

cto_governance/CtoGovernanceDatum.title: CtoGovernanceDatum
cto_governance/CtoGovernanceDatum.description: The datum stored at the CTO Governance anchor UTxO

anyOf.title: AnchorVoteResult
anyOf.description:
Anchor a vote result from Midnight (open relay, T31) — anyone can
 submit this, no signer authorization required by design.

 T111 fix: now requires a real relayer_bond (paid into this
 contract's own value — see this redeemer's real check below) and no
 longer mutates cto_state/community_wallet_hash directly — that now
 only happens via ExecuteProposal, once the challenge window has
 elapsed. See CtoGovernanceDatum's own doc comment on
 pending_relayer_bond for the full reasoning.

anyOf.title: ExecuteProposal
anyOf.description:
Execute a passed proposal (governor or community wallet). T111 fix:
 now also requires the challenge window has elapsed since anchoring,
 and applies the cto_state/community_wallet_hash mutation that used
 to happen immediately in AnchorVoteResult.

anyOf.title: ExpireProposal
anyOf.description: Mark proposal as expired (if not executed within 30 days)

anyOf.title: ClearProposal
anyOf.description: Clear the active proposal (after execution or expiry)

anyOf.title: EmergencySetCommunityWallet
anyOf.description: Emergency: governor sets community wallet (compromise detected)

anyOf.title: VoidPendingProposal
anyOf.description:
T111 fix: governor-only, within the challenge window — voids a
 pending anchor found to be fraudulent (same trust boundary already
 used throughout this codebase for off-chain-verified claims) and
 slashes the relayer's bond, split 60/40 treasury/ops (same ratio as
 every other forfeited bond in this codebase — T33, nhop_challenge.ak,
 cto_sybil_challenge.ak).

anyOf.title: ReclaimRelayerBond
anyOf.description:
T111 fix: the relayer reclaims their bond once their anchored
 proposal has been legitimately Executed or Expired (never voided) —
 permissionless, but the payout destination is fixed by the datum's
 own pending_relayer_key_hash, same "the invariant is the
 authorization" idiom as every other permissionless claim in this
 codebase.

anyOf.title: QueryState
anyOf.description: Read-only query, no state change, no authorization required

cto_governance/ProposalAnchor.title: ProposalAnchor
cto_governance/ProposalAnchor.description: A proposal result anchored from Midnight

fields.title: challenged_voter_key
fields.description:
The Midnight-derived voter public key being accused of secretly being
 the creator (evading creatorVoteCap) — a raw Midnight identity value,
 not a Cardano VerificationKeyHash.

fields.title: challenged_proposal_id
fields.description:
The specific Midnight proposal ID this vote was cast on — ties the
 accusation to one concrete ballot, not a general claim about the
 wallet in the abstract.

fields.title: bond_amount
fields.description:
Must equal the real lovelace locked in this UTXO — enforced
 implicitly by Cardano's balance-conservation rules at resolution,
 same as nhop_challenge.ak.

fields.title: submitted_at
fields.description:
Posix seconds the challenge was submitted — the defense window
 counts from here.

fields.title: evidence_hash
fields.description:
Audit-trail commitment to the off-chain evidence (e.g. a hash of the
 challenger's claimed stake-key-match or tx-graph proof). Not
 independently verified on-chain — see file header.

anyOf.title: ResolveChallenge
anyOf.description:
Governor-only. `current_timestamp` is verified against the tx's real
 validity range (Cardano scripts can't read wall-clock time directly),
 same idiom as every other timestamp check in this codebase.

lp_escrow/DexAction.title: DexAction
lp_escrow/DexAction.description: Which way a proposed whitelist change goes (T30)

lp_escrow/LpEscrowDatum.title: LpEscrowDatum
lp_escrow/LpEscrowDatum.description: The datum stored at the LP escrow UTxO

lp_escrow/LpEscrowRedeemer.title: LpEscrowRedeemer
lp_escrow/LpEscrowRedeemer.description: Redeemer actions — what the contract can be spent with

anyOf.title: SealLock
anyOf.description:
T49: seals the lock (sets lock_timestamp), paired with the bonding
 curve's `Graduate` redeemer in the same transaction. Permissionless —
 verified by `lp_value_received` actually finding `seeded_ada`
 lovelace and `lp_token_amount` of the launch token in this contract's
 own continuing output, not by a governor signature.

anyOf.title: ProposeDexChange
anyOf.description:
T30: propose adding/removing a DEX from the whitelist. Requires
 `multisig_threshold`-of-`multisig_signers` real signatures. Starts
 the 72h public notice clock — does NOT change the whitelist yet.

anyOf.title: ExecuteDexChange
anyOf.description:
T30: apply a pending whitelist change once its 72h notice period has
 elapsed. Permissionless — the proposal was already public for the
 full window, same "deadline is the authorization" idiom as T29's
 ExpireCurve.

anyOf.title: CancelPendingDexChange
anyOf.description: T30: multisig withdraws a pending change before it takes effect.

anyOf.title: Migrate
anyOf.description:
Migrate LP to a whitelisted DEX after lock expiry. Either governor or
 community wallet (if CTO triggered) can call this.

anyOf.title: TriggerCTO
anyOf.description: Trigger CTO — redirect control to community wallet

anyOf.title: DissolveCTO
anyOf.description: Dissolve CTO — return control to governor

anyOf.title: CancelLaunch
anyOf.description: Cancel launch (T29 — bonding curve expired without graduation)

anyOf.title: HarvestFees
anyOf.description:
T13: harvest DEX trading fees without touching the locked LP position.
 Permissionless — like ExpireCurve/ExecuteDexChange/Graduate, the
 invariant checks are the authorization: the locked LP position cannot
 change and the correct recipient must actually be paid, so there is
 nothing a malformed or adversarial caller could gain by invoking this
 incorrectly. Real per-DEX harvest mechanics are NOT modeled here — see
 file header.

anyOf.title: QueryState
anyOf.description: Read-only query, no state change, no authorization required

lp_escrow/LpState.title: LpState
lp_escrow/LpState.description: LP escrow states — mirrors the Midnight PSM enum

lp_escrow/PendingDexChange.title: PendingDexChange
lp_escrow/PendingDexChange.description:
A whitelist change awaiting its 72h public notice period (T30) — at
 most one pending change at a time, deliberately: a second proposal
 can't be queued until the first resolves (executes or is cancelled),
 so there's never ambiguity about which change a given execute call
 applies to.

anyOf.title: ResolveChallenge
anyOf.description:
Governor-only. `current_timestamp` is verified against the tx's real
 validity range (Cardano scripts can't read wall-clock time directly),
 same idiom as every other timestamp check in this codebase.

staking_pool/MerkleProofStep.title: MerkleProofStep
staking_pool/MerkleProofStep.description:
One step of a Merkle inclusion proof — same `(sibling, direction)` shape
 already used by bonding_curve_tier_b.ak's DarkVeil allocation proofs.

staking_pool/StakingPoolDatum.title: StakingPoolDatum
staking_pool/StakingPoolDatum.description:
One per launch. Its own UTXO value holds the real, depleting reward
 token balance.

staking_pool/StakingPoolRedeemer.title: StakingPoolRedeemer
staking_pool/StakingPoolRedeemer.description:
Redeemer actions. Staking itself (first stake, or an additional stake
 by the same wallet) needs no redeemer at all — creating a new Position
 output at this validator's address is permissionless, same as any
 deposit; only spending an existing UTXO here needs validator approval.

anyOf.title: Unstake
ClaimRewards
anyOf.description:
Permissionless — same "the proof is the authorization" idiom as
 bonding_curve.ak's Graduate/ExpireCurve. A real Merkle proof of
 membership for (staker_vkh, claimed_cumulative_amount), plus a hard
 check that payout can only reach staker_vkh's own address, is the
 full authorization; nobody benefits from submitting someone else's
 claim since the funds can't be redirected.

anyOf.title: TopUpPool
anyOf.description:
Creator-only. Adds `amount` of the reward token to the pool's own
 value. No rate recalculation anywhere — see file header.

anyOf.title: PublishRewardRoot
anyOf.description:
Governor-only. Updates reward_root; the pool's own token value and
 claimed_so_far must be unchanged — this redeemer can publish a new
 snapshot but can never move funds.

anyOf.title: QueryState
anyOf.description: Read-only query, no state change.

staking_pool/StakingPositionDatum.title: StakingPositionDatum
staking_pool/StakingPositionDatum.description:
One per stake action. Its own UTXO value holds the staker's real
 staked tokens.

anyOf.title: StartVesting
anyOf.description:
Governor starts vesting — called at graduation. No tokens release
 before this regardless of elapsed wall-clock time.

anyOf.title: ClaimVested
anyOf.description: Creator claims vested tokens

anyOf.title: TriggerCTO
anyOf.description:
Governor triggers CTO — freezes unvested tokens, redirects to
 community treasury

anyOf.title: DissolveCTO
anyOf.description: Governor dissolves CTO — creator returned, resumes vesting

anyOf.title: ClaimCommunityAllocation
anyOf.description:
Phase B fix: permissionless — releases exactly the amount a real,
 passed+executed FundAllocation proposal authorizes, to exactly the
 recipient it names. Amount and recipient are both derived from the
 anchored proposal, never a free redeemer parameter — same reasoning
 as cto_vote_verified's expected_recipient check.

anyOf.title: CancelLaunch
anyOf.description:
Cancel launch (T29 — bonding curve expired without graduation).
 T111 fix: now only reachable from NotStarted — see the redeemer's
 own match-arm comment for why (a launch that already began vesting
 already graduated, so T29's own "expired without graduation"
 rationale no longer applies to it).

anyOf.title: ClaimCancelledAllocation
anyOf.description:
T111 fix: the creator's own never-vested allocation had no recovery
 path once Cancelled — a single governor signature previously froze
 it permanently with zero way out for anyone, including the creator
 themselves. Since CancelLaunch (above) is now only reachable from
 NotStarted, claimed_tokens is always 0 here — nothing was ever
 vested or claimed, so returning the full token_allocation to the
 creator is safe: it's their own carved-out allocation, never
 distributed to or earned by anyone else.

anyOf.title: QueryState
anyOf.description: Read-only query, no state change, no authorization required

zk_anchor/CertificateType.title: CertificateType
zk_anchor/CertificateType.description: Certificate types — which Midnight PSM generated this proof

anyOf.title: DarkVeilCert
anyOf.description:
DarkVeil Fair Launch Certificate (Tier B) — proves: creator bought 0
 tokens, no front-running, >=50% participation

anyOf.title: FullZKCert
anyOf.description:
Full ZK Certificate (Tier C) — proves: all eligibility checks passed,
 private bonding curve was fair

anyOf.title: CtoVoteResult
anyOf.description:
CTO Governance Result Anchor (T31) — anchors the result of a Midnight
 CTO governance vote to Cardano L1

anyOf.title: GraduationCert
anyOf.description:
Bonding Curve Graduation Certificate — proves: 100% sell-through
 achieved, graduation was legitimate

zk_anchor/ZkAnchorDatum.title: ZkAnchorDatum
zk_anchor/ZkAnchorDatum.description: The datum stored at the ZK Anchor UTxO

anyOf.title: AnchorCertificate
anyOf.description: Submit a new ZK certificate (relayer only)

anyOf.title: AddRelayer
anyOf.description: Add an authorized relayer (governor only)

anyOf.title: RemoveRelayer
anyOf.description: Remove an authorized relayer (governor only)

anyOf.title: QueryCertificate
anyOf.description: Read-only query, no state change, no authorization required

anyOf.title: UpdateIpfsCid
anyOf.description:
Update IPFS CID (if the proof bundle needs re-pinning). Only the
 original relayer or governor can do this.
